name: D3.js
summary: Low-level JavaScript library for data-driven document manipulation and visualization using web standards (SVG, Canvas, HTML)

name: Chart.js
summary: Simple yet flexible JavaScript charting library for designers and developers with canvas-based rendering and good defaults

name: ECharts
summary: Feature-rich interactive charting and data visualization library from Apache with declarative API and extensive chart types

name: Highcharts
summary: Commercial JavaScript charting library with extensive chart types, accessibility, and enterprise support; free for non-commercial use

name: Plotly.js
summary: High-level declarative charting library built on D3.js and WebGL with 40+ chart types and interactive features

name: Recharts
summary: Composable React charting library built on D3 with declarative components for building charts from reusable pieces

name: Nivo
summary: Rich set of React dataviz components built on D3 with server-side rendering support and 27+ chart types

name: Victory
summary: React component library for building interactive data visualizations with composable chart components

name: visx
summary: Collection of low-level React visualization primitives from Airbnb combining D3 power with React component model

name: Billboard.js
summary: Re-usable easy-interface JavaScript chart library based on D3.js maintained by NAVER

name: ApexCharts
summary: Modern interactive charting library with clean API, responsive design, and framework wrappers for React/Vue/Angular

name: C3.js
summary: D3-based reusable chart library providing classes for each chart type to simplify D3 chart generation

name: Frappe Charts
summary: Simple responsive modern SVG charts with zero dependencies for quick data visualization

name: uPlot
summary: Ultra-small (~50KB) fast time series chart library optimized for performance with minimal memory footprint

name: Chartist.js
summary: Simple responsive SVG charting library with clean separation of concerns between CSS and JavaScript

name: G2
summary: Concise and progressive visualization grammar from AntV for building interactive data-driven graphics

name: Observable Plot
summary: Concise high-level JavaScript charting library from Observable for exploratory data analysis with sensible defaults

name: Vega
summary: Declarative visualization grammar using JSON specifications to create interactive views of data

name: Vega-Lite
summary: High-level grammar of interactive graphics providing concise JSON syntax that compiles to full Vega specifications

name: Cytoscape.js
summary: Graph theory / network visualization library for analysis and visualization with rich interaction and layout algorithms

name: Sigma.js
summary: WebGL-powered JavaScript library for visualizing large graphs with thousands of nodes and edges performantly

name: vis-network
summary: Dynamic browser-based network visualization library for displaying customizable automatically-organized graph views

name: G6
summary: Graph visualization framework from AntV for interactive relationship data and network analysis with rich layouts

name: Gephi
summary: Desktop application for interactive graph and network visualization, exploration, and analysis of large networks

name: yFiles
summary: Commercial graph visualization SDK with advanced automatic layout algorithms, available for HTML/Java/.NET/JavaFX

name: Graphviz
summary: Open-source graph visualization software using DOT language for describing graphs with automatic layout algorithms

name: react-force-graph
summary: React component for force-directed 2D/3D/VR graph visualization using D3 force simulation and Three.js

name: Dagre
summary: JavaScript library for directed graph layout using Sugiyama-style hierarchical algorithm for DAG visualization

name: ELK
summary: Eclipse Layout Kernel providing automatic graph layout algorithms usable standalone or via elkjs in JavaScript

name: graphology
summary: Robust multi-purpose JavaScript graph library providing graph data structure and standard graph algorithms

name: React Flow
summary: Customizable React component for building interactive node-based editors, flow charts, and diagrams

name: Leaflet
summary: Lightweight open-source JavaScript library for mobile-friendly interactive maps with a simple well-documented API

name: Mapbox GL JS
summary: JavaScript library for interactive vector tile maps using WebGL with custom styling and 3D terrain support

name: OpenLayers
summary: High-performance feature-packed JavaScript library for displaying maps with vector tiles, WMS, WFS, and GeoJSON

name: Deck.gl
summary: WebGL2/WebGPU-powered framework for large-scale data visualization with geospatial and non-geospatial layers

name: CesiumJS
summary: JavaScript library for creating 3D globes and maps with time-dynamic visualization and terrain rendering

name: Turf.js
summary: Advanced geospatial analysis library for browsers and Node.js with modular functions for spatial operations

name: MapLibre GL JS
summary: Open-source community fork of Mapbox GL JS for interactive vector tile maps in the browser using WebGL

name: Kepler.gl
summary: Powerful open-source geospatial analysis tool for large-scale data sets built on Deck.gl and Mapbox GL

name: H3
summary: Hexagonal hierarchical geospatial indexing system from Uber for efficient spatial data partitioning and analysis

name: GeoServer
summary: Open-source Java server for sharing and editing geospatial data implementing OGC WMS/WFS/WCS standards

name: QGIS
summary: Full-featured open-source desktop GIS application for creating, editing, visualizing, and analyzing geospatial data

name: Google Maps API
summary: Google's mapping platform with JavaScript API for embedding interactive maps, geocoding, and routing services

name: draw.io
summary: Free browser-based diagramming tool (also known as diagrams.net) for flowcharts, UML, network diagrams, and more

name: Excalidraw
summary: Virtual whiteboard for sketching hand-drawn style diagrams with real-time collaboration and library support

name: tldraw
summary: Infinite canvas SDK for React with whiteboard and diagramming capabilities; free for non-commercial, paid for production

name: Eraser
summary: AI-powered technical diagramming and documentation tool with diagram-as-code and GitHub integration

name: Three.js
summary: Widely-used JavaScript 3D library providing WebGL rendering with scene graph, cameras, lights, and loaders

name: Babylon.js
summary: Powerful real-time 3D engine using WebGL and WebGPU for rendering interactive 3D experiences in the browser

name: A-Frame
summary: Web framework for building VR/AR experiences using HTML-like declarative components built on Three.js

name: React Three Fiber
summary: React renderer for Three.js enabling declarative 3D scene construction using React components and hooks

name: Spline
summary: Browser-based 3D design tool for creating interactive 3D experiences with real-time collaboration

name: Model Viewer
summary: Web component from Google for rendering 3D models in web pages with AR support and customizable controls

name: PlayCanvas
summary: WebGL/WebGPU game engine with visual editor for creating interactive 3D web content and games

name: PixiJS
summary: Fast 2D WebGL renderer with canvas fallback for creating interactive graphics, games, and animated content

name: AG Grid
summary: Feature-rich JavaScript data grid with community (MIT) and enterprise editions for React/Angular/Vue

name: TanStack Table
summary: Headless UI table library for building powerful tables and datagrids with framework adapters for React/Vue/Solid/Svelte

name: Handsontable
summary: Spreadsheet-like JavaScript data grid component; free for non-commercial use, paid license for commercial projects

name: SlickGrid
summary: Lightning-fast JavaScript grid/spreadsheet with virtual scrolling for rendering millions of rows efficiently

name: DataTables
summary: jQuery plugin for enhancing HTML tables with pagination, searching, sorting, and AJAX data loading

name: Tabulator
summary: Interactive tables and data grids for JavaScript with no dependencies, supporting filtering, sorting, and editing

name: VTK.js
summary: JavaScript implementation of VTK for scientific visualization in the browser including volume rendering and isosurfaces

name: ParaView
summary: Multi-platform data analysis and visualization application for large scientific datasets with parallel processing support

name: Mayavi
summary: 3D scientific data visualization and plotting in Python built on VTK with NumPy integration

name: Bokeh
summary: Interactive visualization library for Python that creates browser-ready plots from large and streaming datasets

name: Altair
summary: Declarative statistical visualization library for Python based on Vega-Lite with a concise grammar of graphics API

name: HoloViews
summary: Python library that makes data analysis and visualization seamless by letting your data visualize itself

name: Plotnine
summary: Grammar of graphics implementation for Python based on R's ggplot2 for creating complex multi-layered plots

name: PyDeck
summary: Python bindings for Deck.gl enabling large-scale geospatial visualization in Jupyter notebooks

name: Folium
summary: Python library to create interactive Leaflet maps from data manipulated with Python (pandas, etc.)

name: GeoPandas
summary: Python library extending pandas with geospatial operations including spatial joins, plotting, and file I/O

name: Datashader
summary: Python library for rendering massive datasets into fixed-size raster images quickly and accurately

name: hvPlot
summary: High-level plotting API for pandas, dask, xarray, and networkx built on HoloViews with familiar .plot() syntax

name: Pygwalker
summary: Python library that turns pandas DataFrames into a Tableau-like visual exploration interface in Jupyter

name: Matplotlib Basemap
summary: Matplotlib toolkit for plotting 2D geographic data on map projections (deprecated in favor of Cartopy)

name: Cartopy
summary: Python library for cartographic projections and geospatial data visualization built on matplotlib and PROJ

name: Shapely
summary: Python library for manipulation and analysis of planar geometric objects based on GEOS library

name: Mosaic
summary: Extensible framework for linking data visualizations, tables, and inputs over DuckDB with cross-filtering

name: Perspective
summary: Interactive analytics and data visualization component for large or streaming datasets with WebAssembly engine

name: Superset
summary: Modern data exploration and visualization platform with rich chart builder and interactive dashboards

name: Tremor
summary: React library for building dashboards with pre-built chart and UI components using Tailwind CSS

name: Apache ECharts for Python (pyecharts)
summary: Python wrapper for Apache ECharts enabling interactive chart generation from Python with Jupyter integration

name: Plotly Express
summary: High-level Python interface to Plotly for creating common figures in a single function call with tidy data

name: Manim
summary: Community-maintained Python library for creating mathematical animations and explanatory videos programmatically

name: Vega Embed
summary: Utility for embedding Vega and Vega-Lite visualizations in web pages with tooltip and export support

name: Mermaid Chart
summary: Commercial SaaS platform for creating, editing, and sharing Mermaid diagrams with visual editor and collaboration

name: PostGIS
summary: Spatial database extension for PostgreSQL adding geographic object support and spatial SQL functions

name: Luma.gl
summary: High-performance WebGL2/WebGPU framework from vis.gl providing GPU-accelerated data visualization primitives

name: Lightweight Charts
summary: Financial charting library from TradingView for rendering lightweight interactive candlestick and time-series charts

name: Leaflet.markercluster
summary: Leaflet plugin for beautiful animated marker clustering with spiderfied click expansion

name: DuckDB-WASM
summary: WebAssembly build of DuckDB enabling in-browser analytical SQL queries over local or remote Parquet/CSV data without a server.

name: TopoJSON
summary: Extension of GeoJSON that encodes topology, reducing file sizes by sharing arc segments between polygons.

name: Mapshaper
summary: Command-line tool and web app for simplifying, editing, and converting geographic data formats (Shapefile, GeoJSON, TopoJSON).

name: GeoJSON
summary: Open standard format for encoding geographic data structures using JSON, widely supported by mapping libraries and tools.
